info.title: DocuPak / DocuFill API
info.description:
API for the **DocuPak** SaaS platform and its embedded **DocuFill** paperwork engine.

## Authentication

| Scope | Scheme | How to obtain |
|-------|--------|---------------|
| Public routes | None | N/A |
| Product portal (SaaS) | `Authorization: Bearer <clerk_jwt>` | Clerk front-end SDK |
| Product portal (API key) | `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_…` | `POST /api/v1/product/auth/api-keys` |
| Internal (WHC staff) | `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` | `POST /api/internal/auth/sign-in` |

## API Key Authentication

External integration partners can authenticate using a long-lived API key instead of a Clerk JWT. API keys:
- Are prefixed with `sk_live_`
- Are stored hashed (SHA-256) — the plaintext is returned only once on creation
- Can be named, listed, and revoked via `/api/v1/product/auth/api-keys`
- Are accepted wherever a Clerk JWT is accepted on product routes

## Base URL
All paths below are relative to `/api/v1`.

productAuth.description: Short-lived Clerk JWT issued by the product portal front-end.

internalAuth.description: Opaque session token issued by `POST /api/internal/auth/sign-in`. WHC staff only.

apiKeyAuth.description: Long-lived API key issued by `POST /api/v1/product/auth/api-keys`. For external integration partners. Keys are prefixed with `sk_live_` and are stored hashed — the plaintext is shown only once on creation.

DocuFillPackage.description: A Docuplete package — a reusable interview template that defines the documents, fields, and configuration for a document workflow.

active.description: Only active packages can have sessions created against them.

DocuFillSessionListItem.description: A session row returned by the session list endpoint. Includes answers, prefill, and PDF URL for integration use (e.g. Zapier).

answers.description: Client answers keyed by field ID. Sensitive fields are not redacted here (use the webhook payload for redacted answers).

prefill.description: Prefill values supplied when the session was created.

generated_pdf_url.description: Relative URL to download the generated PDF. Only present when status is `generated`.

AccountInfo.description: Account and user information returned by the `/product/auth/me` endpoint.

email.description: Email of the authenticated user. `null` when authenticated via API key.

role.description: Role of the authenticated user within the account.

keyPrefix.description: First 16 characters of the key — shown for identification only.

event.description: `interview.submitted` fires on every completed form submission. `interview.test` is sent when you click **Send test** in the dashboard.

package_id.description: Internal ID of the Docuplete package.

token.description: Unique submission token. Use this as an idempotency key — automatic retries deliver the same token.

submitted_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the form was submitted.

answers.description: Key-value map of field IDs to their submitted values. Fields marked sensitive in the package settings are replaced with `[redacted]`.

WebhookDelivery.description: A single webhook delivery attempt recorded in the delivery log.

attempt_number.description: 1 for the initial attempt; increments for each automatic retry (max 4 attempts total: immediate, +5 s, +30 s, +5 min).

http_status.description: HTTP status returned by the endpoint, or null if the request failed to connect.

response_body.description: First 1 KB of the response body, useful for debugging.

post.description:
Sent when a Docuplete interview or customer-link form is completed. Docuplete retries delivery up to 3 times (4 total attempts) with exponential back-off: 5 s → 30 s → 5 min.

Your endpoint must return any **2xx** status within 10 seconds to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses and connection errors trigger a retry.

See the `WebhookPayload` schema and its description for signature verification details.

2XX.description: Return any 2xx to acknowledge receipt. The response body is ignored.

get.description:
Streams a file from the configured public-objects GCS search paths.
The caller must know the exact filename. No authentication required.

parameters.name: filePath
parameters.description:
Relative path of the file (e.g. `logos/sample.png`).
The path may contain forward slashes for nested directories.
OpenAPI 3.x does not natively model wildcard path segments —
this parameter represents the full remainder of the URL path.

200.description: File contents streamed with original Content-Type

get.description:
Returns the logo image for the given account. No authentication required.

The underlying GCS object is stored at a random UUID path so guessing a
valid `accountId` only returns the publicly-intended logo, never a private asset.

404.description: Account not found or no logo configured

get.description: Returns the authenticated organisation's name, logo URL, and brand color.

patch.description:
Partially updates the org's display name and/or accent color.
All fields are optional — omitted fields keep their current values.
Send `clearLogo: true` to remove the current logo.

get.description: Returns the authenticated organisation's name, logo URL, and brand color.

patch.description:
Partially updates the org's display name and/or accent color.
All fields are optional — omitted fields keep their current values.
Send `clearLogo: true` to remove the current logo.

post.description:
Replaces the organisation's logo with the uploaded image.
Upload the raw image binary in the request body and set
`Content-Type` to the image MIME type.

Maximum size: **5 MB**. Accepted types: `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`.

400.description: Unsupported MIME type or empty body

post.description:
Replaces the organisation's logo with the uploaded image.
Upload the raw image binary in the request body and set
`Content-Type` to the image MIME type.

Maximum size: **5 MB**. Accepted types: `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`.

400.description: Unsupported MIME type or empty body

post.summary: Extract brand colors from a website (internal)
post.description:
Fetches the given URL and extracts prominent brand colors from its CSS
and inline styles. Returns an ordered list of hex color strings.

The endpoint refuses private/internal IP ranges (SSRF protection).

200.description: Extracted hex colors ordered by prominence

422.description: Could not fetch or parse the URL

post.summary: Extract brand colors from a website
post.description:
Fetches the given URL and extracts prominent brand colors from its CSS
and inline styles. Returns an ordered list of hex color strings.

The endpoint refuses private/internal IP ranges (SSRF protection).

200.description: Extracted hex colors ordered by prominence

422.description: Could not fetch or parse the URL

get.description: Returns all Docuplete packages configured for the authenticated account, ordered by most-recently updated first.
Returns a single Docuplete package by its numeric ID. The package must belong to the authenticated account.

Returns a paginated list of interview sessions for the authenticated account.
Filter by package, status, or both. Results are ordered newest-first.

When `dealId` is supplied instead, returns the single most-recent session
for that deal (legacy behavior used by the internal portal).

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: Filter sessions to a specific package ID

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of sessions to skip (for pagination)

parameters.name: updatedAfter
parameters.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp. When provided, only sessions updated after this
timestamp are returned. Useful for polling integrations (e.g. Zapier).

parameters.name: cursorId
parameters.description:
Tie-breaker for the `updatedAfter` cursor. When both `updatedAfter` and
`cursorId` are supplied the backend filters using a PostgreSQL tuple
comparison `(updated_at, id) > (updatedAfter, cursorId)`, preventing
missed sessions when multiple rows share the same `updated_at` timestamp.
Results are ordered `updated_at DESC, id DESC` in all cases.

total.description: Total number of sessions matching the filters (before pagination)

post.description:
Creates a new Docuplete interview session for the specified package.
Returns the session record and a bearer token used to load and submit
the public-facing interview form. Sessions expire after **90 days**.

packageId.description: ID of an active Docuplete package

prefill.description: Initial field values to pre-populate in the interview form

token.description: Bearer token passed to the public interview form

interviewUrl.description: Full URL to the public-facing interview form for this session

400.description: Invalid packageId or package not active

post.description:
Creates a new Docuplete interview session for the specified package.
Returns the session record and a bearer token used to load and submit
the public-facing interview form. Sessions expire after **90 days**.

Pass `testMode: true` to create a test session without requiring the
package to be active (requires an authenticated admin account).

packageId.description: ID of an active Docuplete package

dealId.description: Optional deal ID to associate with this session

source.description: Free-text label for how this session was created

prefill.description: Initial field values to pre-populate in the interview

testMode.description: Create a non-production test session (admin only)

token.description: Bearer token passed to the public interview form

400.description: Invalid packageId, scope mismatch, or package not active

get.description: Returns the full interview session by its opaque token. Includes answers, fields, documents, prefill data, and package metadata.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Opaque session token (e.g. `df_abc123`) returned when the session was created

get.description:
Returns the full session object including package metadata, document list,
field definitions, prefill data, and saved answers. No authentication required.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Opaque session token returned when the session was created

patch.description:
Persists a partial or complete answers object for the session. Optionally
updates the session status (e.g. `in_progress`). The session must not have expired.

post.description:
Validates the saved answers, renders each document as a filled PDF,
bundles them into a single packet PDF, and (when configured) uploads
the packet to Google Drive. Returns a download URL for the packet.

The session status is set to `generated`. The session must not have expired.

400.description: Validation failed — required fields are missing or invalid

get.summary: Download the generated packet as PDF
get.description:
Returns the fully-filled packet PDF for display or download.
The packet is rendered on demand from the latest saved answers — it
does not require a prior `/generate` call, but all required fields
must be complete.

patch.description:
Records the current timestamp as `payment_received_at` for the deal.
No request body is needed. Idempotent — calling it again updates the timestamp.

Use `/wire-received` instead when the payment method is a bank wire, as that
endpoint also sets `wire_received_at` and triggers the wire confirmation email.

Records the current timestamp as `wire_received_at` (and also sets
`payment_received_at` if not already set). Sends the wire confirmation
email to the customer — email sending is idempotent (only fires once per deal).
No request body is needed.

200.description: Wire timestamp recorded and confirmation email sent

patch.description:
Stores the carrier tracking number and schedules a shipping notification
email to the customer for 24 hours from now. The `trackingNumber` field
is required and must be non-empty.

get.description:
Returns account and user information for the authenticated session.
Accepts both Clerk JWTs and API keys (`sk_live_...`).

When authenticated via API key, `email` is `null` (keys are account-scoped,
not user-scoped) and `role` is `"member"`.

post.description:
Creates a new API key scoped to the authenticated account.
The full plaintext key is returned **once** and cannot be retrieved again — store it securely.
Keys are prefixed with `sk_live_` and are stored as a SHA-256 hash.

name.description: Human-readable label for the key (max 100 characters).

201.description: API key created. The `key` field contains the plaintext key — shown only once.

key.description: Full plaintext API key. Store securely — not shown again.

get.description: Returns all API keys (active and revoked) for the authenticated account. Plaintext keys are never returned — only the prefix and metadata.

delete.description: Soft-revokes an API key. Revoked keys are rejected immediately. Only keys belonging to the authenticated account can be revoked.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The numeric ID of the API key to revoke.

404.description: Key not found or already revoked
